---
title: "Chicago 311 Dashboard — Static Figures"
format:
  pdf:
    geometry: "margin=1in"
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
import os, json
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import altair as alt
import geopandas as gpd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.gridspec as gridspec
from IPython.display import Image, display

alt.data_transformers.disable_max_rows()

DERIVED = os.path.join("..", "data", "derived-data")
FIG_DIR = os.path.join("..", "figures")
os.makedirs(FIG_DIR, exist_ok=True)

JOHNSON = pd.Timestamp("2023-05-15")
BLUE = "#5B8DB8"
ORANGE = "#D97B53"
PERIOD_LABELS = ["2021–Apr 2023", "May 2023–2024"]
Q_ORDER = ["Q1 (Lowest)", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4", "Q5 (Highest)"]
Q_COLORS = {
    "Q1 (Lowest)": "#c0392b", "Q2": "#e67e22", "Q3": "#f1c40f",
    "Q4": "#27ae60", "Q5 (Highest)": "#2980b9",
}

df = pd.read_parquet(os.path.join(DERIVED, "311_cleaned.parquet"))
df["created_date"] = pd.to_datetime(df["created_date"], errors="coerce")
df["closed_date"] = pd.to_datetime(df["closed_date"], errors="coerce")
df["year_month"] = df["created_date"].dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()
df["period"] = np.where(df["created_date"] < JOHNSON, "2021–Apr 2023", "May 2023–2024")
df["community_area"] = (
    pd.to_numeric(df["community_area"], errors="coerce").astype("Int64").astype(str)
)
if "year" not in df.columns:
    df["year"] = df["created_date"].dt.year

ca = pd.read_csv(os.path.join(DERIVED, "community_area_stats.csv"))
ca["area_numbe"] = ca["area_numbe"].astype(str)
gdf = gpd.read_file(os.path.join(DERIVED, "community_areas.geojson"))
gdf["area_numbe"] = gdf["area_numbe"].astype(str)

# Add community name to df if not already present
if "community" not in df.columns:
    df = df.merge(ca[["area_numbe", "community"]],
                  left_on="community_area", right_on="area_numbe", how="left")

PERIOD_SCALE = alt.Scale(domain=PERIOD_LABELS, range=[BLUE, ORANGE])
Q_SCALE = alt.Scale(domain=Q_ORDER, range=[Q_COLORS[q] for q in Q_ORDER])

rule_df = pd.DataFrame({"date": [JOHNSON]})

def johnson_rule():
    r = alt.Chart(rule_df).mark_rule(
        strokeDash=[5, 4], color="#888", strokeWidth=1.5
    ).encode(x="date:T")
    t = alt.Chart(rule_df).mark_text(
        align="left", dx=5, dy=-8, fontSize=10, color="#666", fontWeight="bold"
    ).encode(x="date:T", text=alt.value("Johnson Inauguration"))
    return r + t

def show_altair(chart, path):
    """Save an Altair chart as PNG and display it."""
    chart.save(path, scale_factor=2)
    display(Image(path))

# Pre-compute community area stats for geography maps
cs_geo = (df.groupby("community_area")
          .agg(total=("sr_type", "count"),
               median_wait=("response_time_days", "median"))
          .reset_index())
cs_geo["community_area"] = cs_geo["community_area"].astype(str)
cs_geo = cs_geo.merge(
    ca[["area_numbe", "community", "population", "median_income", "poverty_rate"]],
    left_on="community_area", right_on="area_numbe", how="left")
cs_geo["per_1k"] = (cs_geo["total"] / cs_geo["population"] * 1000).round(1)
cs_geo["poverty_pct"] = (cs_geo["poverty_rate"] * 100).round(1)

gm_full = gdf.merge(cs_geo, left_on="area_numbe", right_on="community_area", how="left")
gm_full = gm_full.to_crs(epsg=3435)

def plot_choropleth(column, title, cmap, legend_label, figpath):
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 1, figsize=(6, 7))
    gm_full.plot(
        column=column, cmap=cmap, legend=True, ax=ax,
        scheme="quantiles", k=5,
        edgecolor="white", linewidth=0.3,
        missing_kwds={"color": "lightgrey", "label": "No data"},
        legend_kwds={"title": legend_label, "loc": "lower right", "fontsize": 8},
    )
    ax.set_axis_off()
    ax.set_title(title, fontsize=13, fontweight="bold", pad=12)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(figpath, dpi=200, bbox_inches="tight")
    plt.show()
    plt.close()

print(f"Data loaded: {len(df):,} rows, {df['sr_type'].nunique()} service types")
```

## Tab 1 — Overview

### Figure 1: Monthly Request Volume

```{python}
#| label: fig01
vol = (df.groupby(["year_month", "period"]).size()
       .reset_index(name="requests")
       .sort_values("year_month"))

bars = alt.Chart(vol).mark_bar().encode(
    x=alt.X("year_month:T", title="Month"),
    y=alt.Y("requests:Q", title="Number of Requests"),
    color=alt.Color("period:N", scale=PERIOD_SCALE, title="Period"),
    tooltip=[
        alt.Tooltip("year_month:T", title="Month", format="%b %Y"),
        alt.Tooltip("requests:Q", title="Requests", format=","),
        alt.Tooltip("period:N", title="Period"),
    ],
).properties(width=700, height=380, title="Monthly 311 Request Volume by Period")

fig01 = bars + johnson_rule()
show_altair(fig01, os.path.join(FIG_DIR, "fig01_monthly_volume.png"))
```

### Figure 2: Top Service Categories

```{python}
#| label: fig02
tc = (df.groupby("sr_type").size()
      .reset_index(name="requests")
      .nlargest(10, "requests"))

fig02 = alt.Chart(tc).mark_bar(color=BLUE).encode(
    x=alt.X("requests:Q", title="Number of Requests"),
    y=alt.Y("sr_type:N", sort="-x", title="",
            axis=alt.Axis(labelLimit=400)),
    tooltip=[
        alt.Tooltip("sr_type:N", title="Service Type"),
        alt.Tooltip("requests:Q", title="Requests", format=","),
    ],
).properties(width=700, height=380, title="Top 10 Service Categories by Request Count")
show_altair(fig02, os.path.join(FIG_DIR, "fig02_top_categories.png"))
```

### Figure 3: Service Mix Over Time (Top 4)

```{python}
#| label: fig03
top4 = df["sr_type"].value_counts().head(4).index.tolist()
comp = (df[df["sr_type"].isin(top4)]
        .groupby(["year_month", "sr_type"]).size()
        .reset_index(name="requests")
        .sort_values("year_month"))

lines = alt.Chart(comp).mark_line(strokeWidth=2).encode(
    x=alt.X("year_month:T", title="Month"),
    y=alt.Y("requests:Q", title="Number of Requests"),
    color=alt.Color("sr_type:N", scale=alt.Scale(scheme="set2"), title="Service Type"),
    tooltip=[
        alt.Tooltip("year_month:T", title="Month", format="%b %Y"),
        alt.Tooltip("sr_type:N", title="Service Type"),
        alt.Tooltip("requests:Q", title="Requests", format=","),
    ],
).properties(width=700, height=380, title="Service Request Mix Over Time (Top 4 Categories)")

fig03 = lines + johnson_rule()
show_altair(fig03, os.path.join(FIG_DIR, "fig03_service_mix.png"))
```

## Tab 2 — Response Times

### Figure 4: Response Time Shift (Faster vs Slower)

```{python}
#| label: fig04
pre_s = df[df["period"] == "2021–Apr 2023"].groupby("sr_type")["response_time_days"].median()
post_s = df[df["period"] == "May 2023–2024"].groupby("sr_type")["response_time_days"].median()
chg = (post_s - pre_s).dropna().reset_index()
chg.columns = ["service_type", "change_days"]
chg["count"] = df.groupby("sr_type").size().reindex(chg["service_type"]).values
chg = chg[chg["count"] > 500].sort_values("change_days")
chg["direction"] = chg["change_days"].apply(
    lambda x: "Faster" if x < -1 else ("Slower" if x > 1 else "Stable"))
show = pd.concat([chg.head(8), chg.tail(8)]).drop_duplicates()

shift_bars = alt.Chart(show).mark_bar().encode(
    x=alt.X("change_days:Q", title="Change in Median Wait (days)"),
    y=alt.Y("service_type:N",
            sort=alt.EncodingSortField(field="change_days", order="ascending"),
            title="", axis=alt.Axis(labelLimit=400)),
    color=alt.Color("direction:N",
                    scale=alt.Scale(
                        domain=["Faster", "Stable", "Slower"],
                        range=["#27ae60", "#bbb", "#c0392b"]),
                    title="Direction"),
    tooltip=[
        alt.Tooltip("service_type:N", title="Service Type"),
        alt.Tooltip("change_days:Q", title="Change (days)", format=".1f"),
        alt.Tooltip("direction:N", title="Direction"),
    ],
).properties(width=700, height=max(380, len(show) * 26),
             title="Response Time Shift: Which Services Got Faster or Slower?")

zero = alt.Chart(pd.DataFrame({"x": [0]})).mark_rule(
    color="#333", strokeWidth=1
).encode(x="x:Q")

fig04 = shift_bars + zero
show_altair(fig04, os.path.join(FIG_DIR, "fig04_response_shift.png"))
```

### Figure 4b: Single Service Response Time Drill-Down

```{python}
#| label: fig04b
# Use the top service type as the representative "selected" service
chosen = df["sr_type"].value_counts().index[0]
svc = df[df["sr_type"] == chosen]
sm = (svc.groupby(["year_month", "period"])["response_time_days"]
      .median().reset_index()
      .sort_values("year_month"))
sm.columns = ["month", "period", "median_wait"]

drill_line = alt.Chart(sm).mark_line(strokeWidth=2.5, point=True).encode(
    x=alt.X("month:T", title="Month"),
    y=alt.Y("median_wait:Q", title="Median Response Time (days)"),
    color=alt.Color("period:N", scale=PERIOD_SCALE, title="Period"),
    tooltip=[
        alt.Tooltip("month:T", title="Month", format="%b %Y"),
        alt.Tooltip("median_wait:Q", title="Median Response Time (days)", format=".1f"),
        alt.Tooltip("period:N", title="Period"),
    ],
).properties(width=700, height=380,
             title=f"Response Time Drill-Down — {chosen}")

fig04b = drill_line + johnson_rule()
show_altair(fig04b, os.path.join(FIG_DIR, "fig04b_drill_down.png"))
```

## Tab 3 — Equity & Income

### Figure 5: Median Wait by Income Quintile & Period

```{python}
#| label: fig05
f_q = df[df["income_quintile"].notna()].copy()
eq = (f_q.groupby(["income_quintile", "period"])["response_time_days"]
      .median().reset_index())
eq.columns = ["income_quintile", "period", "median_wait"]

fig05 = alt.Chart(eq).mark_bar().encode(
    x=alt.X("income_quintile:N", title="Income Quintile",
            sort=Q_ORDER, axis=alt.Axis(labelAngle=0)),
    y=alt.Y("median_wait:Q", title="Median Wait (days)"),
    color=alt.Color("period:N", scale=PERIOD_SCALE, title="Period"),
    xOffset="period:N",
    tooltip=[
        alt.Tooltip("income_quintile:N", title="Income Quintile"),
        alt.Tooltip("period:N", title="Period"),
        alt.Tooltip("median_wait:Q", title="Median Wait (days)", format=".1f"),
    ],
).properties(width=700, height=400,
             title="Median Wait by Income Quintile & Administration Period")
show_altair(fig05, os.path.join(FIG_DIR, "fig05_quintile_wait.png"))
```

### Figure 6: Service Type × Income Quintile Heatmap

```{python}
#| label: fig06
top10 = f_q["sr_type"].value_counts().head(10).index.tolist()
hd = (f_q[f_q["sr_type"].isin(top10)]
      .groupby(["sr_type", "income_quintile"])["response_time_days"]
      .median().reset_index())
hd.columns = ["service_type", "income_quintile", "median_wait"]
hd["service_label"] = hd["service_type"].str[:30]

heat = alt.Chart(hd).mark_rect().encode(
    x=alt.X("income_quintile:N", title="Income Quintile", sort=Q_ORDER),
    y=alt.Y("service_label:N", title="Service Type",
            sort=alt.EncodingSortField(field="median_wait", op="mean", order="descending"),
            axis=alt.Axis(labelLimit=400)),
    color=alt.Color("median_wait:Q",
                    scale=alt.Scale(scheme="orangered"),
                    title="Median Wait (days)"),
    tooltip=[
        alt.Tooltip("service_type:N", title="Service Type"),
        alt.Tooltip("income_quintile:N", title="Income Quintile"),
        alt.Tooltip("median_wait:Q", title="Median Wait (days)", format=".1f"),
    ],
).properties(width=600, height=max(340, len(top10) * 36),
             title="Response Time Heatmap: Service Type × Income Quintile")

text = alt.Chart(hd).mark_text(fontSize=11, color="white").encode(
    x=alt.X("income_quintile:N", sort=Q_ORDER),
    y=alt.Y("service_label:N",
            sort=alt.EncodingSortField(field="median_wait", op="mean", order="descending")),
    text=alt.Text("median_wait:Q", format=".0f"),
)

fig06 = heat + text
show_altair(fig06, os.path.join(FIG_DIR, "fig06_heatmap.png"))
```

### Figure 7: Request Volume by Income Quintile

```{python}
#| label: fig07
vol_q = f_q.groupby("income_quintile").size().reset_index(name="requests")

fig07 = alt.Chart(vol_q).mark_bar().encode(
    x=alt.X("income_quintile:N", title="Income Quintile",
            sort=Q_ORDER, axis=alt.Axis(labelAngle=0)),
    y=alt.Y("requests:Q", title="Number of Requests"),
    color=alt.Color("income_quintile:N", scale=Q_SCALE, title="Income Quintile", legend=None),
    tooltip=[
        alt.Tooltip("income_quintile:N", title="Income Quintile"),
        alt.Tooltip("requests:Q", title="Requests", format=","),
    ],
).properties(width=500, height=350, title="Request Volume by Income Quintile")
show_altair(fig07, os.path.join(FIG_DIR, "fig07_volume_quintile.png"))
```

## Tab 4 — Geography

### Figure 8a: Median Response Time by Community Area

```{python}
#| label: fig08a
plot_choropleth(
    "median_wait", "Median Response Time by Community Area",
    "OrRd", "Median Wait (days)",
    os.path.join(FIG_DIR, "fig08a_choropleth_wait.png")
)
```

### Figure 8b: Total Requests by Community Area

```{python}
#| label: fig08b
plot_choropleth(
    "total", "Total 311 Requests by Community Area",
    "YlOrRd", "Total Requests",
    os.path.join(FIG_DIR, "fig08b_choropleth_total.png")
)
```

### Figure 8c: Requests per 1,000 Residents by Community Area

```{python}
#| label: fig08c
plot_choropleth(
    "per_1k", "311 Requests per 1,000 Residents by Community Area",
    "YlOrBr", "Requests per 1K Residents",
    os.path.join(FIG_DIR, "fig08c_choropleth_per1k.png")
)
```

### Figure 8d: Median Household Income by Community Area

```{python}
#| label: fig08d
plot_choropleth(
    "median_income", "Median Household Income by Community Area",
    "Greens", "Median Income ($)",
    os.path.join(FIG_DIR, "fig08d_choropleth_income.png")
)
```

### Figure 8e: Poverty Rate by Community Area

```{python}
#| label: fig08e
plot_choropleth(
    "poverty_pct", "Poverty Rate by Community Area",
    "Reds", "Poverty Rate (%)",
    os.path.join(FIG_DIR, "fig08e_choropleth_poverty.png")
)
```

## Tab 5 — Neighborhoods

### Figure 9: Top 20 Neighborhoods by Request Volume

```{python}
#| label: fig09
ns = (df.groupby(["community", "income_quintile"])
      .agg(total=("sr_type", "count"),
           med_wait=("response_time_days", "median"),
           pct30=("response_time_days", lambda x: (x > 30).mean() * 100))
      .reset_index()
      .sort_values("total", ascending=False)
      .head(20))
ns["med_wait"] = ns["med_wait"].round(1)
ns["pct30"] = ns["pct30"].round(1)
ns.columns = ["Neighborhood", "Income Quintile", "Requests", "Median Wait (days)", "% Over 30 Days"]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 7))
ax.axis("off")

col_widths = [0.30, 0.18, 0.13, 0.20, 0.19]
table = ax.table(
    cellText=ns.values,
    colLabels=ns.columns,
    colWidths=col_widths,
    loc="center",
    cellLoc="center",
)
table.auto_set_font_size(False)
table.set_fontsize(8.5)
table.scale(1, 1.6)

# Style header
for j in range(len(ns.columns)):
    table[0, j].set_facecolor("#2c3e50")
    table[0, j].set_text_props(color="white", fontweight="bold")

# Alternating row shading
for i in range(1, len(ns) + 1):
    color = "#f2f2f2" if i % 2 == 0 else "white"
    for j in range(len(ns.columns)):
        table[i, j].set_facecolor(color)

ax.set_title("Top 20 Neighborhoods by 311 Request Volume",
             fontsize=13, fontweight="bold", pad=16)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(os.path.join(FIG_DIR, "fig09_neighborhoods_table.png"), dpi=200, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.show()
plt.close()
```

### Figure 10a: Top Services in Most-Requested Neighborhood

```{python}
#| label: fig10a
# Pick the most-requested neighborhood as the representative example
top_nbr = (df.groupby("community").size()
             .sort_values(ascending=False)
             .index[0])
nbr = df[df["community"] == top_nbr]

svc_n = (nbr.groupby("sr_type").size()
         .reset_index(name="requests")
         .nlargest(8, "requests"))

fig10a = alt.Chart(svc_n).mark_bar(color=ORANGE).encode(
    x=alt.X("requests:Q", title="Requests"),
    y=alt.Y("sr_type:N", sort="-x", title="", axis=alt.Axis(labelLimit=400)),
    tooltip=[
        alt.Tooltip("sr_type:N", title="Service Type"),
        alt.Tooltip("requests:Q", title="Requests", format=","),
    ],
).properties(width=700, height=320,
             title=f"Top Services in {top_nbr} (Most-Requested Neighborhood)")
show_altair(fig10a, os.path.join(FIG_DIR, "fig10a_nbr_top_services.png"))
```

### Figure 10b: Monthly Request Trend in Most-Requested Neighborhood

```{python}
#| label: fig10b
nm = (nbr.groupby(["year_month", "period"]).size()
      .reset_index(name="requests")
      .sort_values("year_month"))

trend = alt.Chart(nm).mark_bar().encode(
    x=alt.X("year_month:T", title="Month"),
    y=alt.Y("requests:Q", title="Requests"),
    color=alt.Color("period:N", scale=PERIOD_SCALE, title="Period"),
    tooltip=[
        alt.Tooltip("year_month:T", title="Month", format="%b %Y"),
        alt.Tooltip("requests:Q", title="Requests", format=","),
        alt.Tooltip("period:N", title="Period"),
    ],
).properties(width=700, height=320,
             title=f"Monthly Request Trend — {top_nbr}")

fig10b = trend + johnson_rule()
show_altair(fig10b, os.path.join(FIG_DIR, "fig10b_nbr_monthly_trend.png"))
```

## Summary

```{python}
#| label: summary
for f in sorted(os.listdir(FIG_DIR)):
    if f.endswith(".png"):
        size = os.path.getsize(os.path.join(FIG_DIR, f)) / 1024
        print(f"  {f:50s} {size:6.0f} KB")
```
